volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 5.3.1132b - Section 5.4.1197)

sections.label: Section 5.3.1132b
sections.original: Quid ibi faciunt ? dic mihi .
sections.translation: What are they doing there ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1133
sections.original: Aphrodisia hodie Veneris est festus dies :
sections.translation: To-day is the Aphrodisia , the festive day of Venus ! they have gone there to entreat the Goddess to be propitious to them .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1135
sections.original: Pol satis scio , impetrarunt , quando hic hic adest .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , they ’ ve fully prevailed , I ’ m sure , inasmuch as he has arrived here .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1136
sections.original: Eho an huius sunt illae filiae ?
sections.translation: How now , are these his daughters ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1136b
sections.original: Ita ut praedicas .
sections.translation: Just as you say . ( To HANNO .) Your kindness has clearly come to our rescue , in your having arrived here to-day at the very time ; for this day their names were to have been changed [*] ,

sections.label: Section 5.3.1140
sections.original: facerentque indignum genere quaestum corpore .
sections.translation: and they were to have made a livelihood , disgraceful to their station , by their persons .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1141
sections.original: Auamma illi .
sections.translation: Haudones illi [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1141b
sections.original: Hauon bane silli in mustine .
sections.translation: Havon bene si illi , in mustine .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1142
sections.original: Mepstaetemes tas dum et alanna cestimim .
sections.translation: Me ipsi et eneste dum et alamna cestinum [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1143
sections.original: Quid illí locuti sunt inter se ? dic mihi .
sections.translation: What is it they are saying among themselves ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1144
sections.original: Matrem hic salutat suam , haec autem hunc filium .
sections.translation: He ’ s saluting his mother , and she this her son .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1145
sections.original: tace atque parce muliebri supellectili .
sections.translation: Hold your peace , and let alone the woman ’ s gear .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1146
sections.original: Quae east supellex ?
sections.translation: What gear is that ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1146b
sections.original: Clarus clamor .
sections.translation: Loud talking

sections.label: Section 5.3.1146c
sections.original: Sine modo .
sections.translation: without limit .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1147
sections.original: Tu abduc hosce intro ; et una nutricem simul
sections.translation: ( To MILPHIO .) Do you lead these people in-doors ( pointing to his SERVANTS .) and bid this nurse to come away together with you to your house .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1148b
sections.original: Fac quod imperat .
sections.translation: Do as he requests .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1149
sections.original: Sed quís illas tibi monstrabit ?
sections.translation: But who ’ s to point them out to you ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1149b
sections.original: Ego doctissume .
sections.translation: I will , right skilfully .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1150
sections.original: Abeo igitur .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go away then .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1150b
sections.original: Facias modo quam memores mavelim .
sections.translation: I ’ d only rather that you would do so , than say so .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1151
sections.original: patruo advenienti cena curetur volo .
sections.translation: I wish a dinner to be got ready for my kinsman on his arrival .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1152
sections.original: Lachanna vos , quos ego iam detrudam ad molas ,
sections.translation: Lachananim [*] you ! ( aside to the SERVANTS .) whom I ’ ll just now be packing off to the mill-stones , and from there after that to the dungeon and the oaken log [*] . I ’ ll give you reason to praise your treatment here but slightly .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1155
sections.original: Audin tu , patrue ? dico , ne dictum neges :
sections.translation: Do you hear , kinsman ? I say , don ’ t you revoke what has been said ; promise me your elder daughter in marriage .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1157
sections.original: Pactam rem habeto .
sections.translation: Consider the thing as agreed on .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1157b
sections.original: Spondesne igitur ?
sections.translation: Do you promise her , then ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1157c
sections.original: Spondeo .
sections.translation: I do promise her .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1158
sections.original: Mi patrue , salve . nam nunc es plane meus .
sections.translation: My kinsman , blessings on you ! for now you are mine beyond a doubt ; now at length shall I converse with her without restraint .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1160
sections.original: nunc , patrue , si vis tuas videre filias ,
sections.translation: Now , kinsman , if you wish to see your daughters , follow me .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1161b
sections.original: Iamdudum equidem cupio , et te sequor .
sections.translation: Why , really , this long time I ’ ve been longing for it , and I ’ ll follow you .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1162
sections.original: Quid si eámus illis obviam ?
sections.translation: What if we go and meet them ?

sections.label: Section 5.3.1162b
sections.original: At ne intervias
sections.translation: But I ’ m afraid lest we should pass them on the road . Great Jupiter , do now reinstate my fortunes for me as being certain instead of uncertain !

sections.label: Section 5.3.1165
sections.original: † Ego quidem meos amores mecum confido fore .
sections.translation: I trust that my charmer will be my own . But look , I catch sight of them .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1166b
sections.original: Haecine meae sunt filiae ?
sections.translation: What , are these my daughters ? How tall from being such little creatures have they now become !

sections.label: Section 5.3.1167b
sections.original: Scin quid est ?
sections.translation: Do you know how it is ? These are Grecian columns [*] ; they are wont to be erect .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1169
sections.original: Opino hercle hodie , quod ego dixi per iocum ,
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I do think that this day the very thing that I said by way of joke

sections.label: Section 5.3.1170
sections.original: id eventurum esse et severum et serium ,
sections.translation: will be coming to pass both soberly and seriously ,

sections.label: Section 5.3.1171
sections.original: ut haec ínveniantur hodie esse huius filiae .
sections.translation: that these will be discovered to-day to be his daughters .

sections.label: Section 5.3.1172
sections.original: Pol istúc quidem iam certum est . tu istos , Milphio ,
sections.translation: Troth , that very thing is quite certain now . Do you , Milphio , take them ( pointing to the SERVANTS ) in-doors ; we ’ ll wait here for these damsels .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1174
sections.original: Fuit hódie operae pretiúm cuivis qui amábilitati animum adiceret ,
sections.translation: ’ Twas worth the while , to-day , of him who has a taste for loveliness

sections.label: Section 5.4.1175
sections.original: oculís epulas dare , délubrum qui hodie órnatum eo visére venit .
sections.translation: to afford a feast to his eyes , in coming hither to the Temple this day to see the sights . Upon my faith , I was charmed there to-day with the most elegant offerings of the courtesans , worthy of Venus , the most handsome Goddess ; nor did I despise her worship this day ; so great an abundance of beauteous objects was there there , each nicely arranged in its own place .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1179
sections.original: arás tus , murrinus , ómnis odor complébat . haud sordére visust
sections.translation: The odours of Arabia and of myrrh filled everything . The festive day seemed to be affected with no gloom , Venus , nor did thy Temple ; so great a throng of her dependants was there , who had come to Venus of Calydon .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1181a
sections.original: Certo enim , quod quidem ad nos duas
sections.translation: But certainly , as far indeed as regarded us two , sister , we were all-powerful in our prayers , beauteous and gainers of her favour ; neither were we there held in ridicule by the young men , which , i ’ faith , sister , happened to all the rest .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1184
sections.original: Malim ístuc aliis vídeatur , quam uti tú te , soror , conlaúdes .
sections.translation: I ’ d rather that it should so appear to other persons , than that you , sister , should praise yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1185
sections.original: Spero équidem .
sections.translation: Indeed , I trust so .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1185b
sections.original: Et pól ego , quom , íngeniis quibus súmus atque aliae , gnósco ;
sections.translation: Troth , and so do I , when I reflect of what breeding we and the others are . We were born in that station , that it befits us to be unblemished by faultiness .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1187
sections.original: Iuppíter , qui genus colis álisque hominum , per quém vivimus vitálem aevom ,
sections.translation: Jupiter , who dost preserve and feed the race of men , through whom we pass this mortal life , in whose hands are the hopes of life in all men , prithee , do grant this day as a prosperous one

sections.label: Section 5.4.1189
sections.original: † rebus meis agundis , ut quíbus annos multós carui quasque é patria
sections.translation: for my fortunes ! Those whom I ’ ve missed for many years ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.1190
sections.original: perdidi parvas † redde is libertatem , invictae praemium ut esse sciam pietati .
sections.translation: and whom when little I lost from their native land , to them restore their liberty , that I may be sure that for an indomitable sense of duty there is a reward .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1191
sections.original: Omnía faciet Iuppíter faxo , nam mi ést obnoxius ét me metuít .
sections.translation: I ’ ll engage that Jove shall do it all ; for to me he is indebted [*] , and stands in awe of me .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1191b
sections.original: Tace quaeso .
sections.translation: Prithee , do hold your peace .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1191a
sections.original: Ne lácruma , patrue .
sections.translation: Kinsman , do not weep .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1192
sections.original: Vt vólup est homini , méa soror , si quód agit cluet victória ;
sections.translation: As it is a pleasure for a man , my sister , if he succeeds in anything , to have the credit of victory , just so did we this day among the rest excel them all in beauty .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1194
sections.original: Stultá , soror , magis es quám volo . an tu éo pulchra videre , óbsecro ,
sections.translation: Sister , you are more silly than I could wish . Prithee , do you really think yourself a beauty ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.1195
sections.original: si tíbi illi non os óblitumst fulígine ?
sections.translation: if your face has not been besmeared with soot [*] ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1195b
sections.original: O pátrue , o patrue mí .
sections.translation: O kinsman ! O kinsman , dearest of all kinsmen [*] to me !

sections.label: Section 5.4.1195c
sections.original: Quid est ,
sections.translation: What is it , son of my cousin ? My son , tell me , what is it you wish ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1196b
sections.original: At enim hóc volo agas .
sections.translation: Why , really , I do wish you to attend to this .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1196c
sections.original: At enim ágo istuc .
sections.translation: Why , really , I am attending to it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1197
sections.original: O pátrue mi patruíssime .
sections.translation: Kinsman , kinsman , dearest of all kinsmen to me !
